summary: The CMA issued final redeterminations for five water companies that referred their PR24 Final Determinations. The CMA awarded £463m of additional revenues (17% of the £2.7bn sought) and increased the appointee WACC from 4.03% to 4.20%.

companies.name: Anglian Water
key_changes: Reduced enhancement allowances vs company request
Additional revenues awarded relative to Ofwat FD

companies.name: Northumbrian Water
key_changes: No change to total allowances overall — less for base costs but more for financing costs

companies.name: South East Water
key_changes: Water-only company — bills do not include wastewater costs

companies.name: Southern Water
key_changes: Reduced enhancement allowances vs company request
Additional revenues awarded relative to Ofwat FD

companies.name: Wessex Water
key_changes: Additional revenues awarded relative to Ofwat FD

key_conclusions: The CMA awarded only 17% of the additional revenue the companies sought, rejecting 83% of the combined £2.7bn request.
The most significant change was the cost of capital, increased from 4.03% to 4.20% (appointee, CPIH-real), reflecting updated market data.
The average expected bill increase across the five companies was 2.2%, nearly half driven by market movements.
Enhancement allowances were increased for Wessex, South East and Northumbrian, but reduced for Anglian and Southern.
SVT accepted the Ofwat FD and was not part of the CMA process. SVT retains the 4.03% WACC.

glossary_terms.definition: A process where the Competition and Markets Authority re-examines and re-sets the price controls originally determined by Ofwat, following a referral by a water company.
The Weighted Average Cost of Capital applied at the appointee (regulated entity) level, combining wholesale and retail controls.
